textPlain: It'd be interesting to see what percentage of companies signed up with Larry stick with Larry out of sheer momentum. Sure, proprietary DBMSes can be faster and more efficient, but I can't imagine they'd be so much more efficient as to justify the license fee, so something else would have to be the justification.

It's for the support in most cases.But last time I really used Oracle's RDBMS (10g era) it still had capabilities that no open source database had. If you really needed that, there wasn't an easy substitute. I'm sure Postgres has narrowed the gap by now.
